tools.name: store_memory
tools.description: Store information in persistent memory. Automatically extracts entities (people, organizations, locations) and facts from the content.

content.description: The text content to memorize. Can be facts, context, decisions, meeting notes, preferences, etc.

ttl.description: Optional time-to-live. Examples: "24h", "7d", "30d", "1y", or omit for permanent storage.

tools.name: recall_memories
tools.description: Search persistent memory for relevant information using hybrid search (semantic + keyword). Use this BEFORE answering questions about past decisions, context, people, or projects.

query.description: Natural language query. Can be a question or keywords.

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return (1-50, default: 5)

tools.name: forget_memories
tools.description: Delete memories from persistent storage. GDPR-compliant deletion. Provide exactly one of: memory_id, entity, or all_memories.

memory_id.description: Delete a specific memory by its ID

entity.description: Delete all memories about a specific entity (person, org, etc)

all_memories.description: Set to true to delete ALL memories. Use with extreme caution!

tools.name: health_check
tools.description: Check Remembra server health and connection status. Returns server version, status, and component health.

tools.name: ingest_conversation
tools.description: Ingest a conversation and automatically extract memories. Processes messages and intelligently extracts facts worth remembering long-term.

messages.description: List of message dicts with 'role' and 'content'. Optional: 'name', 'timestamp'.

session_id.description: Optional session ID for grouping related conversations

store.description: If false, returns extraction results without storing (dry run)

resources.name: Recent Memories
resources.description: Returns the 10 most recently stored memories for quick context
